data.title: Origin Bancorp Boosts Dividend Despite Slight Earnings Decline
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Origin Bancorp ( (OBK) ) has issued an update.

Origin Bancorp reported first-quarter 2026 net income of $27.7 million, or $0.89 per diluted share, down from $29.5 million, or $0.95 per diluted share, in the fourth quarter of 2025, while pre-tax, pre-provision earnings held essentially flat at $40.2 million. Net interest income reached a record $87.2 million as net interest spread improved and loans and deposits expanded, but annualized ROAA slipped to 1.11%, and credit metrics showed modest increases in past-due and nonperforming loans.

The bank’s Optimize Origin efficiency and balance sheet initiative, launched in January 2025, continued to underpin a strategy aimed at delivering top-quartile returns, with management reaffirming a near-term ROAA run-rate target of at least 1.15% by the fourth quarter of 2026. Reflecting confidence in balance sheet strength and earnings durability, the board in April 2026 approved a 67% increase in the quarterly common dividend to $0.25 per share and the company repurchased 165,500 shares during the quarter, actions that signal an ongoing commitment to shareholder returns despite a slightly lower earnings run rate.

The most recent analyst rating on (OBK) stock is a Buy with a $48.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Origin Bancorp stock, see the OBK Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on OBK Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, OBK is a Outperform.

OBK scores well primarily due to a strengthened balance sheet and a positive earnings-call outlook with clear growth and ROA targets. Technicals are supportive but mixed near-term, while valuation is only moderate with a modest dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on OBK stock, click here.

More about Origin Bancorp

Origin Bancorp, Inc., headquartered in Ruston, Louisiana, is a bank holding company for Origin Bank and operates in the commercial and consumer banking industry. The company focuses on loans held for investment, including commercial and industrial, commercial real estate and multifamily residential real estate lending, supported by a growing deposit base across its markets.

Average Trading Volume: 209,277

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

data.title: Horizon Bancorp Reports Strong First Quarter 2026 Results
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Horizon Bancorp ( (HBNC) ) is now available.

Horizon Bancorp, Inc. on April 22, 2026 reported unaudited first quarter 2026 results that underscored resilient profitability, with annualized return on average assets of 1.62%, return on average tangible common equity of 19.02% and net interest margin of 4.29%, while net income reached $26.2 million, or $0.51 per diluted share, slightly below the prior quarter but above the year-ago core run rate. The quarter featured robust core deposit growth of 11.3% annualized, driven by 22.8% annualized growth in non-interest-bearing deposits, modest 4.0% annualized commercial loan growth, strong credit quality with net charge-offs at 0.05% of average loans, and tightly managed expenses at $40.7 million, reinforcing management’s message of stable performance and solid capital generation despite a volatile macroeconomic backdrop.

Horizon’s balance sheet metrics highlighted ample funding for future loan growth, as elevated interest-earning cash balances from strong deposit inflows modestly compressed net interest margin by about four basis points in the quarter, reflecting a strategic decision to preserve pricing discipline rather than chase lower-yielding residential mortgages. With book value per share continuing to advance and core profitability remaining in the top tier of peers, the results suggested the bank is sustaining its competitive positioning in community banking while maintaining flexibility to support disciplined asset growth and long-term shareholder returns.

The most recent analyst rating on (HBNC) stock is a Buy with a $20.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Horizon Bancorp stock, see the HBNC Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on HBNC Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, HBNC is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by 2025 financial statement deterioration (losses and negative margins) and weak price/indicator trends (below major moving averages with negative MACD). Offsetting factors include constructive 2026 guidance with strong NIM momentum and a supportive dividend yield, alongside improved leverage and better operating/free cash flow versus earnings.

To see Spark’s full report on HBNC stock, click here.

More about Horizon Bancorp

Horizon Bancorp, Inc., based in Michigan City, Indiana, is the parent of Horizon Bank and operates a high quality community banking model focused on relationship-based commercial lending, core deposit gathering and traditional retail banking services across its footprint. The company emphasizes disciplined organic growth, strong credit quality and peer-leading capital generation to drive long-term shareholder value within the regional banking sector.

data.title: Pathward Financial Posts Solid Q2 Results Amid Tax Strength
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Pathward Financial ( (CASH) ) has provided an announcement.

Pathward Financial, Inc., a U.S.-based financial holding company focused on tax services, partner solutions and commercial finance, reported fiscal second-quarter 2026 net income of $72.9 million, or $3.35 per share, for the three months ended March 31, 2026, slightly down from $75.0 million a year earlier. Management highlighted strong tax-season performance, robust partner pipeline and growing commercial finance net interest income, while Pathward, N.A. again earned Great Place To Work certification, underscoring its emphasis on culture.

For the 2026 fiscal second quarter, total revenue rose to $276.3 million, driven by a 9% increase in noninterest income, with notable gains in card and deposit fees, tax-related fee income and refund transfer product fees, which together made noninterest income 55% of revenue. New loan originations excluding tax services climbed to $1.31 billion, supported by a 2025 consumer finance contract, and the company posted a 3.56% annualized return on average assets and 54.41% return on average tangible equity.

During the six months ended March 31, 2026, tax-services product revenue increased 13% to $95.7 million, with higher refund advances and transfers and a $4.4 million reduction in credit-loss provisions, lifting net tax-services product income 30% to $62.0 million; refund advance originations reached $1.87 billion versus $1.66 billion in the 2025 season. These gains reflect efforts to grow tax products, capture market share and refine underwriting models and data analytics in this core business line.

Net interest income for the quarter fell 8% to $125.1 million, mainly due to lower income from the consumer finance portfolio and cash, following the October 2025 sale of a consumer portfolio that had carried higher reported yields, while commercial finance loans and leases generated an $8.4 million interest-income increase. Average interest-earning assets slipped to $7.65 billion as the balance sheet shifted away from cash and securities toward loans and leases, and net interest margin narrowed to 6.63%, or 5.32% on an adjusted basis excluding gross consumer finance yields, with funding costs remaining low.

Noninterest income rose 9% to $151.2 million, aided by stronger tax-related fees, card and deposit fees and refund transfer revenues, partially offset by weaker secondary market and rental income after a prior-year working-capital portfolio gain. Servicing fees on custodial deposits at partner banks increased both sequentially and year over year on higher average balances, underscoring the importance of Pathward’s partner-bank arrangements and deposit-driven revenue streams.

Noninterest expense declined 3% to $143.5 million, reflecting lower card processing and other costs, even as the company increased spending on compensation, benefits, buildings and software to support its long-term strategy. Card processing expense, which is tied to rate-indexed partner agreements and federal funds rate moves, remained significant but decreased year over year, and the company’s effective tax rate for the quarter was 16.2% on income tax expense of $14.2 million, reinforcing solid bottom-line profitability despite portfolio repositioning.

The most recent analyst rating on (CASH) stock is a Buy with a $107.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Pathward Financial stock, see the CASH Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CASH Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CASH is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by strong underlying fundamentals (high profitability and strong returns with currently low leverage) and positive technical momentum with the stock trading above key moving averages. A raised FY2026 EPS guide and improving operating metrics further support the outlook, while historical free-cash-flow inconsistency and execution/timing risks (partner ramp and tax season) temper the score.

To see Spark’s full report on CASH stock, click here.

More about Pathward Financial

Pathward Financial, Inc., listed on Nasdaq as CASH, is a U.S.-based financial holding company focused on powering financial inclusion through a mix of tax services, commercial and consumer finance, card and deposit solutions, and partner-driven banking platforms. Its subsidiary Pathward, N.A. operates as a nationally chartered bank, and the group emphasizes partner solutions, higher-returning assets, and workplace culture, having been Certified by Great Place To Work for four consecutive years.

Average Trading Volume: 267,805

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $2.17B

On April 22, 2026, Essential Properties Realty Trust reported first-quarter 2026 results marked by robust acquisition and capital-raising activity, alongside double-digit growth in key cash flow metrics. The REIT invested $388.6 million across 126 properties at a 7.7% weighted average cash cap rate, realized $10.2 million of disposition proceeds from six assets, and lifted adjusted funds from operations per share by 11% to $0.50, even as net income per share slipped 3% to $0.28.

On April 17, 2026, AiRWA Inc. held its 2026 annual meeting of stockholders, with 19,689,058 of 42,142,432 eligible common shares represented, establishing a quorum for voting. Shareholders elected five directors to serve until the next annual meeting, ratified Enrome LLP as independent auditor for the fiscal year ending April 30, 2026, and approved executive compensation along with holding future say-on-pay votes every three years.

OIO Group ( (OIO) ) has provided an announcement.

On April 21, 2026, OIO Group received approval from Nasdaq for the anticipated closing of its previously announced business combination with De Tomaso Automobili Holdings Limited and the listing of the combined company on the Nasdaq Capital Market, subject to customary conditions. The company plans to consummate the deal around April 24, 2026, when the merged entity is expected to begin trading under the ticker OIO, supported by a 1-for-3 reverse stock split designed to satisfy Nasdaq’s minimum bid price and other initial listing requirements.

The reverse stock split will take effect immediately before the business combination closing, consolidating every three outstanding ordinary shares into one and forming a key part of OIO Group’s transaction and listing structure. Alongside assurances that its shareholder distribution will meet Nasdaq criteria, the company has published unaudited pro forma condensed combined financial information to illustrate the post-merger financial profile, signaling a significant step in its transition to a restructured, Nasdaq-compliant issuer.

BCE ( (TSE:BCE) ) has provided an update.

BCE Inc. reported on April 21, 2026 the results of shareholder elections to convert between its fixed-rate Series AG and floating-rate Series AH preferred shares, effective May 1, 2026. After these conversions, the company will have 9,375,684 Series AG shares and 2,832,114 Series AH shares outstanding, both continuing to trade on the Toronto Stock Exchange.

For the five-year period starting May 1, 2026, Series AG preferred shares will pay a fixed quarterly dividend at an annual rate of 5.30%, while Series AH will maintain a monthly floating dividend tied to the prime rate and an adjustment factor linked to market pricing. The announcement clarifies BCE’s future preferred share capital structure and dividend terms, providing income-oriented investors with transparent options between fixed and floating yield exposure.

FS Bancorp ( (FSBW) ) has issued an update.

FS Bancorp, Inc., the parent of 1st Security Bank of Washington, provides a mix of commercial, consumer and real estate lending, deposit products, cash management services and home mortgage origination across its Commercial and Consumer Banking and Home Lending segments. The bank maintains a sizable loan portfolio in commercial real estate and consumer lending, and continues to build out its geographic reach, including projected expansion into the Portland, Oregon market.

On April 21, 2026, FS Bancorp reported first-quarter 2026 net income of $7.8 million, or $1.02 per diluted share, slightly below both the prior quarter and the year-ago period, though pre-tax income rose 4.6% year-over-year to $9.9 million. The board declared its 53rd consecutive quarterly cash dividend of $0.29 per share, supported by stable core deposits of $2.31 billion, 4.9% annual loan growth, a record split-adjusted book value per share of $42.42, stronger Home Lending production and solid regulatory capital ratios, while share repurchases underscored ongoing capital return to investors.

Management highlighted the announced February merger with Pacific West Bancorp and anticipated 2026 entry into the Portland, Oregon market as strategic growth drivers. Segment results showed $6.7 million in net income from Commercial and Consumer Banking and $1.1 million from Home Lending, with improved mortgage activity and disciplined deposit pricing contributing to earnings resilience despite a higher effective tax rate and softness in consumer loan balances.

Greene County Bancorp ( (GCBC) ) has provided an update.

On April 22, 2026, Greene County Bancorp, Inc. announced that its board approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.10 per share on its common stock for the quarter ended March 31, 2026, maintaining an annualized rate of $0.40 per share, unchanged from the prior quarter. The dividend will be paid on May 29, 2026, to shareholders of record as of May 15, 2026, signaling continued capital returns and stability in the company’s payout policy.

Greene County Bancorp, MHC, the federal mutual holding company that owns 54.1% of the outstanding common shares, will waive its right to receive this dividend, following Federal Reserve nonobjection to waive dividends of up to $0.64 per share across four quarters in 2026. This waiver concentrates the cash distribution among public minority shareholders while allowing the company to preserve capital at the majority-owner level, which may support balance-sheet flexibility and stakeholder confidence in the community bank’s financial management.

data.title: Maison Solutions Enacts Reverse Stock Split to Retain Nasdaq Listing
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Maison Solutions Inc. Class A ( (MSS) ) has provided an announcement.

Maison Solutions Inc., a U.S.-based specialty grocery retailer offering traditional Asian food and merchandise to Asian-American communities, operates supermarkets under the HK Good Fortune banner in Los Angeles and Lee Lee International Supermarket in the Phoenix and Tucson metro areas. Since its 2019 formation, the company has focused on providing culturally attuned fresh food and daily necessities to diverse neighborhoods in major Western U.S. markets.

On April 13, 2026, Maison Solutions’ board approved a 1-for-10 reverse stock split of its Class A common stock, which became effective at 12:01 a.m. Eastern Time on April 24, 2026 and was announced publicly on April 22, 2026. The move, previously authorized by a stockholder vote on October 19, 2025, reduced outstanding shares from about 28.8 million to roughly 2.9 million, is aimed at lifting the share price to meet Nasdaq’s $1.00 minimum bid requirement, and leaves ownership percentages, authorized share counts and par values for common and preferred stock essentially unchanged.

Following the reverse split, Maison Solutions’ stock continues to trade on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol MSS, now under a new CUSIP, with fractional shares rounded up to the nearest whole share to avoid cash-in-lieu payments. The consolidation also triggered proportional adjustments to outstanding warrants, convertible securities and stock incentive plans, a step designed to preserve investor economic positions while supporting the company’s effort to maintain its Nasdaq listing and access to public capital markets.

The most recent analyst rating on (MSS) stock is a Buy with a $4.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Maison Solutions Inc. Class A stock, see the MSS Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on MSS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, MSS is a Neutral.

The score is weighed down primarily by weak financial performance (losses, negative cash flow, and high leverage) and a clearly bearish technical backdrop (below all major moving averages with negative MACD). Valuation provides limited support due to negative earnings and no dividend, while the Nasdaq compliance event adds additional downside risk.

To see Spark’s full report on MSS stock, click here.

More about Maison Solutions Inc. Class A

Maison Solutions Inc. is a U.S.-based specialty grocery retailer focused on traditional Asian food and merchandise, serving primarily Asian-American communities. Founded in 2019, the company holds equity interests in supermarkets operating as HK Good Fortune in the Los Angeles area and Lee Lee International Supermarket in the Phoenix and Tucson, Arizona markets.

Its stores emphasize Asian fresh produce, meat, seafood and daily necessities tailored to traditional family values while adapting to younger, faster-paced lifestyles. Maison Solutions aims to meet the needs of culturally diverse neighborhoods where it operates, positioning itself as a niche player in the growing ethnic and specialty grocery segment.

data.title: UBS Warns Swiss Capital Overhaul Could Force USD 37 Billion in Extra CET1 After Credit Suisse Deal
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

ETRACS Alerian MLP Index ETN Class B ( (AMUB) ) has provided an announcement.

On April 22, 2026, UBS sharply criticized new Swiss regulatory capital measures, arguing that the finalized Capital Adequacy Ordinance and proposed Banking Act changes are extreme, out of line with international standards and potentially damaging to the Swiss economy. The bank said it is still reviewing the government’s materials and will give further detail with its first‑quarter 2026 results on April 29.

Under the new ordinance, UBS’s capitalized software will be amortized over a maximum of three years for capital purposes and prudential valuation adjustments will rise, changes that together are expected to remove about USD 4 billion of net CET1 capital at group level and USD 2 billion at UBS AG, cutting the group CET1 ratio by roughly 0.8 percentage points once fully implemented between 2027 and 2029. Separately, the proposal to fully deduct foreign participations from UBS AG’s standalone CET1, phased in over seven years, would force the bank to hold around USD 20 billion in additional CET1 capital, lifting the de facto minimum CET1 ratio at group level to about 18.4% and, after CAO‑related derecognition, to roughly 17.6%.

UBS said these new requirements would come on top of around USD 15 billion of extra CET1 capital already needed because of the Credit Suisse takeover, taking total additional CET1 demands to about USD 37 billion and implying annual capital costs of some USD 3 billion. The bank warned this would further understate its capital strength versus peers and cited an independent BAK Economics study suggesting the proposed full deduction of foreign participations alone could cost Switzerland up to CHF 34 billion in cumulative GDP over ten years, alongside lower investment, employment and tax revenues.

Despite the looming changes, UBS is maintaining its targets of an underlying 15% return on CET1 capital and a sub‑70% cost/income ratio by end‑2026, and it reaffirmed its planned 2026 capital returns. The group said it will continue to support fact‑based parliamentary deliberations, seek ways to protect shareholders while limiting any impact on clients and employees, and press for recognition of the economic and competitive risks posed by further tightening an already tough Swiss capital framework.

More about ETRACS Alerian MLP Index ETN Class B

UBS Group AG, headquartered in Zurich and Basel, is Switzerland’s largest global wealth manager and a leading domestic bank, with complementary investment banking and asset management operations. The group, which recently absorbed Credit Suisse, operates under one of the world’s most stringent capital regimes and plays a central role in Switzerland’s financial system and broader economy.

Average Trading Volume: 612
